title: [skip ci] EMERGENCY: Revert "Cirrus: Enable labeling of EC2 VMs"
body:
Seeing CI failures in VM.keepalive:

   ERROR: Failed to update one or more image timestamps: fedora-aws-etc

This reverts commit 56e7b511e1d1568809e7b4066b97adb2d498fed1.

Signed-off-by: Ed Santiago <santiago@redhat.com>
```release-note
None
```

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *edsantiago* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [edsantiago] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

Maybe it's just coincidence and I'm overreacting. If so, shrug, re-commit that PR another day when things are less frantic.

Link to [failed task](https://cirrus-ci.com/task/6547815736803328)
Okay that's really odd.  My update didn't touch the `VM img. keepalive` task, and it's operating on AMI's not instances.  I think this must be some other coincidental failure, investigating...

...found it!  The image is in the "recycle bin", placed there `Thu Apr 06 2023 19:28:46 GMT-0400` (EDT).  Though I can't find any record of it being deleted by an automated image-prune job :confused: I'll restore the image to get CI un-stuck, and continue investigating how/why this happened.

Update: [It was deleted by this pruning job](https://cirrus-ci.com/task/5972461808779264?logs=main#L12).  I also noticed that the image is completely missing a timestamp tag - which explains why it got pruned.  I'll temporarily stop all automatic image-pruning until I get a fix in place.
